data.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Sub-Agent Blocker

#### Expected Behavior

`REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md` Step 4 overlay dispatch instructions should document that `FINDINGS_OUTPUT` must be set to `<WORKTREE_ARTIFACTS>/reviewer-findings-test-quality.json` when dispatching `dso:code-reviewer-test-quality`. Per the reviewer's own SKILL.md (lines 274-282):

```bash
if [[ -n "${FINDINGS_OUTPUT:-}" ]]; then
    _OUTPUT_FLAG="--output $FINDINGS_OUTPUT"
else
    _OUTPUT_FLAG="--output $WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR/reviewer-findings.json"
fi
```

Without `FINDINGS_OUTPUT`, the reviewer writes to `reviewer-findings.json` (the tier file) instead of `reviewer-findings-test-quality.json` (the overlay file). The `record-review.sh` OVERLAY_MISSING check then fails.

#### Actual Behavior

When `dso:code-reviewer-test-quality` was dispatched with only `WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR` set (as documented in `per-worktree-review-commit.md`), the reviewer wrote to `reviewer-findings.json` instead of `reviewer-findings-test-quality.json`. `record-review.sh` reported: `OVERLAY_MISSING: classifier flagged overlay(s) [test-quality] but no reviewer-findings-<dim>.json file recorded`.

#### Error Message
```
OVERLAY_MISSING: classifier flagged overlay(s) [test-quality] for diff_hash f4ca4f4c...
but no reviewer-findings-<dim>.json file recorded for them. Overlay dispatch was skipped.
Re-run /dso:review and ensure every flagged overlay agent is dispatched in parallel with
the tier reviewer (REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4).
```

#### Fix Required

`REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md` Step 4 overlay dispatch block (and `per-worktree-review-commit.md` Step 2 dispatch block) must document that orchestrators must set `FINDINGS_OUTPUT=<WORKTREE_ARTIFACTS>/reviewer-findings-test-quality.json` in the dispatch prompt so the test quality reviewer writes to the correct overlay filename.

#### Workaround Applied

Set `FINDINGS_OUTPUT: /tmp/workflow-plugin-19f23804c0019122/reviewer-findings-test-quality.json` explicitly in dispatch prompt. This caused the reviewer to use `--output $FINDINGS_OUTPUT` and write to the correct file.
data.title: [REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md]: overlay dispatch section omits FINDINGS_OUTPUT requirement -> test quality reviewer writes to wrong filename
